title: rhynchosia difformis
intro: Rhynchosia difformis, also known as the hairy bean or climbing bean, is a perennial vine species in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rhynchosia difformis include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe digestive problems and reduce pain and swelling. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional herbal preparations, such as poultices and ointments.
